description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a distinctive pattern of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves reveal a contrasting lighter stone texture beneath a dark, almost black, surface layer, creating a striking striped appearance. The block's sides are solid and unadorned, with a consistent dark coloration, while the top surface displays a marbled effect in the lighter areas. The grooves are deep and regular, suggesting a functional or decorative purpose, possibly to provide traction or drainage. The block appears dense and heavy, likely carved from a single piece of stone or constructed from solid stone material. Its robust and geometric form makes it suitable for use as an urban barrier, step, or decorative element in public spaces.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, revealing a marbled light-and-dark striped pattern. The sides are solid and dark, emphasizing the striking contrast of the grooved top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the block's solid dark face and the horizontal grooves on the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and regular spacing of the vertical grooves, with the marbled stone visible between them.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the solid dark face and the horizontal grooves on top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the vertical grooves and the alternating light and dark pattern on the top.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with no signs of erosion or discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
